content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
P.o. Box ;S07 • ...j.2,s J Street. Sacram .... nto. Co\. 950! 2-0X07 
(916) ,-:;22-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886 
James Lewis 
County of Santa Clara 
Office of the County Counsel 
70 West Hedding Street 
9th Floor, East Wing 
San Jose, California 9511 0-1770 
Re: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-99-009 
Dear Mr. Lewis: 
February 17, 1999 
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the Santa Clara County 
Board of Supervisors regarding the mass mailing provisions ofthe Political Reform Act (the 
"ACt'-').l 
QUESTION 
May the Santa Clara County's Environmental Resources Agency include the names and 
districts of its supervisors on the inside cover of its guide to the county's solid waste services? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The Santa Clara County's Environmental Resources Agency may list the names and 
districts of each county supervisor on the inside cover of the guide since it qualifies forthe 
exception for a roster listing in subdivision (b)(l) of Regulation 18901. Of course, you must 
otherwise comply with Regulation 18901. 
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 _ 
18995. of the California Code of Regulations. 


FACTS 
File No. A-99-009 
Page 2 
Santa Clara County's Environmental Resources Agency publishes infonnationai 
brochures. You would like to list the name and district of each county supervisor on the inside 
cover of the county's guide to solid waste services. You would list the names under a heading 
such as "Santa Clara County Board of Supervisors." The names would be in identical typeface 
and no photographs or signatures would be included. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 89001 states that no mass mailing shall be sent at public expense. Regulation 
18901 interprets that statutory section. You ask whether a listing of your county supervisors in a 
guide to the county's solid waste services in the same typeface and without the photographs or 
signatures of the supervisors would fit within the exception listed in subdivision (b )(8) of 
Regulation 18901. That subdivision states that a mass mailing of the following item is not 
prohibited: 
"A telephone directory, organization chart, or similar listing or 
roster which includes the names of elected officers as well as other 
individuals in the agency sending the mailing. where the name of 
each elected officer and individual listed appears in the same type 
size, typeface, and type color. Such item may not include an 
elected officer's photograph, name, signature, or any other 
reference to an elected officer, except as specifically pennitted in 
this subdivision (b )(8) or elsewhere in this regulation." [Emphasis 
added.] 
The roster listing does not comply with subdivision (b )(8) since the roster listing does not 
include "other indi viduals in the agency sending the mailing." However, the roster listing does 
meet the criteria of subdivision (b)(1). The subdivision states that a mass mailing of the 
following item is not prohibited: 
"Any item in which the elected officer's name appears only in the 
letterhead or the logotype of the stationery, fonns (including "For 
Your Infonnation" or "Compliments of'cards, and envelopes of 
the agency sending the mailing, or of a committee of the agency, or 
of the elected officer, or in a roster listing containing the names of 
all elected officers of the agency. In any such item, the names of 
all elected officers must appear in the same type size, typeface, 
type color, and location. Such item may not include the elected 


File No. A-99-009 
Page 3 
officer's photograph, signature, or any other reference to the 
elected officer, except as specifically pennitted in this subdivision 
(b )(1) or elsewhere in this regulation." [Emphasis added.] 
A roster listing in lieu of letterhead naming the county board of supervisors in a mass 
mailing sent out by the county meets the requirements of this exemption.2 (McCormack Advice 
Letter, No. A-90-497.) Of course, the mass mailing must otherwise comply with Regulation 
18901. 
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 
(916) 322-5660. 
SGC:MC:tls 
Sincerely, 
Steven G. Churchwell 
General Counsel 
By: 
Marte Castaiios 
Staff Counsel, Legal Division 
2 A general reference to the board is is not a reference to an individual board member. (Owen Advice 
Letter, No. 1-90-704.)

districts of each county supervisor on the inside cover of the guide since it qualifies forthe 
exception for a roster listing in subdivision (b)(l) of Regulation 18901. Of course, you must 
otherwise comply with Regulation 18901.
